Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze an event log of 5 cases against a normative process description I need to identify which cases deviate from the described process and explain the violations based on the text

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Normative Process Description Start Always begins with Receive order Step 2 Check inventory must occur next Branch 1 Sufficient Inventory Sequence Receive Check Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Constraints Authorize payment after Check inventory and before packaging Package items after authorization and before shipping Ship order after packaging never after cancellation Close order is always the final activity Branch 2 Insufficient Inventory Sequence Receive Check Cancel order Close order Constraints No fulfillment activities Package Ship after cancellation Close order is final General Constraints Check inventory before Authorize payment or Cancel order Authorize payment before Package items Package items before Ship order Ship order never after Cancel order Close order must be the last activity in all cases Any deviation from these general principles such as shipping after cancellation authorizing payment before checking inventory or packaging items before authorization would be considered a violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Event Log Case by Case Case 1 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close Matches Branch 1 exactly All constraints satisfied No violations Case 2 Sequence Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close Package items occurs before Authorize payment Constraint violated Package items should only occur after authorization Violation Incorrect order of Package and Authorize Case 3 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close After Cancel order it proceeds to Ship order Constraint violated Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Violation Shipping after cancellation inclusion of fulfillment steps after cancellation Case 4 Sequence Receive Check Cancel Close Matches Branch 2 exactly No violations Case 5 Sequence Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Package items is completely missing goes directly from Authorize payment to Ship order Constraint violated Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Violation Missing Package items step before shipping

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Structure Output List each case that violates the process For each violating case explicitly state the violation s and reference the normative description Case 1 Compliant Case 2 Violates order of Package vs Authorize Case 3 Violates by shipping after cancellation performing fulfillment after cancellation Case 4 Compliant Case 5 Violates by skipping packaging step Ensure explanations directly quote or paraphrase the constraints from the prompt Check for ambiguities Ill stick to the explicit constraints given Draft structure Introduction Summary Case-by-case analysis focusing on violations Refine explanations Case 2 The sequence is Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close The normative process explicitly states that Authorize payment should occur before packaging Package items should only occur after authorization Here packaging happens before authorization violating this constraint Case 3 The sequence is Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close The description clearly states that once an order is canceled it is illogical to continue with activities intended for fulfillment and that Ship order should never occur after a cancellation This case violates the process by shipping an already canceled order and including fulfillment steps post-cancellation Case 5 The sequence is Receive Check Authorize Ship Close The normative flow for sufficient inventory requires Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order This case omits the Package items activity entirely skipping a mandatory step between authorization and shipping

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self-Correction Verification during thought Case 2 Package before Authorize Text says Authorize payment should only occur before packaging Violation confirmed Case 3 Cancel then Ship Text says Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Violation confirmed Case 5 Missing Package Text says Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Skipping Package is a deviation Violation confirmed Check against prompt Close order should be the final activity in all scenarios All cases end with Close order so thats fine All aligns well Self-Correction Refinement during drafting Wait lets double check Case 5 Is skipping a step considered a deviation The prompt says Any deviation from these general principles Skip Package breaks the Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order rule So yes its a violation Case 2 Package before Authorize Explicitly violates Package items should only occur after authorization Case 3 Ship after Cancel Explicitly violates never after a cancellation All solid
